text.transcription: 歲次七十八己巳年四月十日將駕

transliterations.transliteration: suè 次 Tshit Tsa̍p Pat kī 巳 liân sù Gua̍t Tsa̍p li̍t Tsiong 駕
seoi3 ci3 cat1 sap6 baat3 gei2 zi6 nin4 sei3/si3 jyut6 sap6 jat6 zoeng1/zoeng3 gaa3
suì cì qī shí bā jǐ sì nián sì yuè shí rì jiāng jià

trans.div: The 78 year of the Republic of China will begin on April 10th

Penghu County Government Civil Affairs Department Funeral Management Section
馬公敬老樹歷史碑
Magong Stele For The Old Tree
Magong Zhonghua Str. Rongshu Xia (Under the Banyan Tree)
馬公中華路榕樹下
building churches facing east
der Bau von Kirchen mit der Absis in Richtung Osten
setting up tombs facing east
Gräber werden so errichtet, dass sie nach Osten zeigen
building churches with a floor plan in the form of a cross
der Bau von Kirchen mit einem kreuz-förmigen Grundriss
馬公孤拔和部下的墓的紀念碑
Magong Memorial of the Tomb of Admiral Amedee Courbet and His Officers

description.div: The first site we visited on Penghu. It indicates the place where the tomb of Admiral Courbet is supposed to have been. A copy of the tomb is now located at Shetou Shan (Snakehead Hill) near Fenggui, Magong. While the body of Admiral Courbet had been relocated in 1885 to France, the bodies of his officers were relocated in 1954 to the French Graveyard in Keelung. It can be assumed that the photos of the graveyard taken during the Japanese period show the original gravemarkers. The biggest marker, presumably that of Admiral Courbet is in a style similar to that we find with graveyardmakers of the 19th century in the Hong Kong Cemetery for mainly British citizens. Who designed and built the gravemarkers in this site is not known to us. The copy found at Shetou Shan and which might integrate some original slabs, is made of granite, a stone not found on Penghu. It thus might be the case that the entire marker has been shipped to Penghu. A comparison with French colonial tombs in Vietnam might be of interest to shed some light on this marker.

geography.address: 台灣澎湖馬公長安北甲

馬公觀音亭的拜鐘

Temple Bell of Magong Tianhou Gong (Mazu Miao)
Side altar in side temple Shengshantang 省善堂

description.div: Side altar in side temple Shengshantang 省善堂

names.name: The outer space of Magong Guanyin Ting

description.div: The outer space of Magong Guanyin Ting

A garden behind a condo in Magong. The garden opens up to other gardens and free spaces such as parking lots and a street. The audio-recording thus captures a wide range of sounds that are typical for a residential area in Magong.

names.name: Table of the side altar in side temple Shengshantang 省善堂

description.div: Table of the side altar in side temple Shengshantang 省善堂

běi jiǎ běi chén gōng wǔ yíng zhā jìng

description.div: Offerings, consiting of fresh cut flowers, a bag of dried chrysanthemum flowers as used for making infusions (herbal teas) and an orange.
